questions.question_text: Arkansas real estate licenses are renewed on what cycle?
questions.explanation: Arkansas real estate licenses are renewed on an annual cycle. Licensees must complete 7 hours of CE before renewal.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to stop practicing but keep their license. What status should they request?
questions.explanation: Licensees not actively practicing may place their license on inactive status. Reactivation requires meeting any CE deficiencies that accrued during inactive status.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Firm A to join Firm B. What must occur?
questions.correct_answer: AREC must be notified of the change
distractors: The salesperson must retake the state exam
questions.explanation: Licensees must notify AREC of any change in principal broker affiliation. A transfer requires proper documentation but does not require re-examination.

questions.question_text: What is the annual renewal fee for an Arkansas salesperson license?
questions.explanation: The salesperson renewal fee is $60 (brokers pay $80) when filed by the September 30 deadline; late renewal costs more. The $50 fee is the original license application fee. A $25 Recovery Fund fee is paid at initial licensure; at renewal a fund fee is assessed only if needed to restore the fund to $250,000.

questions.question_text: A licensee on inactive status wants to return to active practice. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: Complete any CE deficiencies from inactive period
distractors: Complete 60 hours of pre-license education again
questions.explanation: Reactivating an inactive license requires the licensee to satisfy any CE hours that were missed during inactive status. Re-examination is not required.

questions.question_text: What must every Arkansas real estate firm maintain?
questions.correct_answer: A definite place of business with signage
distractors: A physical office in every county served
A separate office for each licensee
questions.explanation: Each firm must maintain a definite place of business identified by signage displaying the principal broker's name and firm name.

questions.question_text: A licensee changes their home address. What should they do?
questions.correct_answer: Notify AREC of the address change promptly
distractors: No action needed until next renewal
Obtain written approval from their broker first
questions.explanation: Licensees must notify AREC of any change in name, address, or contact information. Business address changes must also be reported promptly.

questions.question_text: A broker opens a new branch office. Which requirement applies?
questions.correct_answer: The office must display firm identification signage
distractors: The branch must have its own firm license
The principal broker must relocate there
Branch offices are prohibited in Arkansas
questions.explanation: Every place of business, including branch offices, must be identified by proper signage. A branch office where licensees are assigned must also have a Designated Executive Broker in charge.

questions.question_text: A licensee changes their legal name due to marriage. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: Notify AREC of the name change
distractors: Pass the licensing exam under the new name
questions.explanation: Any change in licensee information, including legal name changes, must be reported to AREC. A new license number is not required.

questions.question_text: An Arkansas firm operates without any signage identifying its name. This violates:
questions.explanation: Every firm must maintain a definite place of business with proper signage. Operating without firm identification signage is a violation of the place of business requirements.

questions.question_text: A licensee is convicted of a felony. What must they do regarding AREC?
distractors: Nothing, unless it involves real estate fraud
Wait until their next license renewal to disclose
Only report if sentenced to prison time
questions.explanation: Licensees must report criminal convictions (other than traffic violations) to AREC in writing within 30 days. Failure to report is itself a violation of the license law. This applies whether or not the crime involves real estate.

questions.question_text: A salesperson witnesses another licensee mishandling trust funds. What is the salesperson's obligation?
distractors: Report only to their principal broker
questions.explanation: Licensees must report knowledge of violations by other licensees to AREC. Failure to report is itself a violation of the license law.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is itself a violation of Arkansas license law?
questions.correct_answer: Failing to report known violations by others
questions.explanation: Licensees are required to report violations by other licensees. Failure to report is a separate violation that can result in disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: A licensee receives discipline from another state's real estate commission. What must they do in Arkansas?
questions.correct_answer: Report the out-of-state action to AREC
distractors: Nothing, other state actions do not apply
File an appeal with Arkansas circuit court
questions.explanation: Disciplinary actions from other states must be reported to AREC. Out-of-state discipline can be grounds for AREC to take its own disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: Engaging in real estate activities without a license is a violation of:
questions.correct_answer: The Arkansas Real Estate License Law
questions.explanation: Unlicensed real estate activity violates the Arkansas Real Estate License Law (Title 17, Chapter 42). AREC can investigate and prosecute such violations.
